top_signals.title: UK Gas Prices Extend Gains
top_signals.summary: UK natural gas prices climbed past 130 pence per therm on Wednesday, the highest level in more than three months, as escalating tensions in the Middle East heightened concerns over LNG supplies. President Donald Trump warned that the US would target Iranian bridges and power infrastructure next week unless Tehran returns to negotiations. His warning ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Asian Stocks Fall as Kospi Hit by Selloff in Chips: Markets Wrap
top_signals.summary: Semiconductor stocks tumbled across Asia, dragging regional equities lower, as investors became more skeptical that the artificial intelligence-driven rally can withstand lofty valuations. Oil climbed.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Five Oil and Gas Stocks Ready for a Hormuz Spike and a Hawkish Fed
top_signals.summary: Two shocks are hitting energy investors at once, and they point in opposite directions.  The first is obvious. Iran’s Revolutionary Guard declared the Strait of Hormuz closed again on July 11, the U.S. answered with three straight nights of strikes, and roughly a fifth of the world’s seaborne oil trade is once more moving in a trickle or not at all.  Brent punched back above $86 this week, a one-month high, and crude is up around 40 percent since January.  Repeated U.S. bombing has not broken Tehran’s grip on the…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Grain shipments jump 13%, but weaker outlook ahead
top_signals.summary: “Global grain shipments have increased 13% y/y so far this year, supported by strong wheat, maize and soya bean exports from the Americas and Europe. Improved harvests across most major exporters have boosted their export surpluses, driving higher shipments to Asia, the Middle East and North Africa,” says Filipe Gouveia, Shipping Analysis Manager at BIMCO. ...

top_signals.title: World Cup gave bars and restaurants a needed boost as consumers flash warning signs, Fed says
top_signals.summary: The latest edition of the Fed's Beige Book indicated that the soccer tournament wasn't necessarily translating to broader economic growth.

top_signals.title: UNESCO panel to urge Japan to reform exhibits at gold mines on Sado Island
top_signals.summary: The draft was compiled based on South Korea's argument that people from the Korean Peninsula were forced to provide labor at the mining complex during World War II.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: DTCC, Wall Street’s post-trade powerhouse, tests tokenized markets with industry heavy hitters
top_signals.summary: The DTCC is pushing to bring tokenization of real world assets from concept to reality using existing financial infrastructure.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Personal Income and Outlays, April 2026
top_signals.summary: Personal income decreased less than $0.1 billion (less than 0.1 percent at a monthly rate) in April, according to estimates released today by the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA). Disposable personal income (DPI)-personal income less personal current taxes-decreased $19.9 billion (0.1 percent), and personal consumption expenditures (PCE) increased $111.1 billion (0.5 percent). Personal outlays-the sum of PCE, personal interest payments, and personal current transfer payments-increased $114.0 billion in April. Personal saving was $611.7 billion in April, and the personal saving rate-personal saving as a percentage of DPI-was 2.6 percent. Full Text
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Ship Recycling Market Still Soft
top_signals.summary: The ship recycling market has remained soft over the past week. In its latest weekly report, Best Oasis (www.best-oasis.com), a leading cash buyer of ships, said that “the Indian recycling market, which remained soft during the previous week, weakened further this week amid continued subdued buying interest. The limited availability of suitable tonnage has restricted ...

top_signals.title: ADM completes first B100 biofuel trial voyage
top_signals.summary: US-based Archer Daniels Midland (ADM) has completed its first trial voyage using B100 (100% biofuel) onboard its bulk carrier MV Harvest Rain. The vessel was bunkered with approximately 107 mt of B100 before undertaking a five-day trial off the coast of Brazil, during which the company evaluated the fuel’s performance across storage, purification, engine operation ...

top_signals.title: EU and India strengthen strategic partnership with third Trade and Technology Council
top_signals.summary: European Commission Press release Brussels, 15 Jul 2026 Today, the European Union and India held the third meeting of their Trade and Technology Council (TTC) in Brussels, reaffirming the TTC as a central platform for cooperation on trade, technology and security.
